columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time when the event was generated

columns.name: Id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the audit record

columns.name: RecordType
columns.description: The type of operation indicated by the record

columns.name: CreationTime
columns.description: The date and time in UTC when the user performed the activity

columns.name: Operation
columns.description: The name of the user or admin activity

columns.name: OrganizationId
columns.description: The GUID for your organization's Microsoft 365 tenant

columns.name: UserType
columns.description: The type of user that performed the operation

columns.name: UserKey
columns.description: An alternative ID for the user identified in the UserId property

columns.name: Workload
columns.description: The Microsoft 365 service where the activity occurred

columns.name: ResultStatus
columns.description: Indicates whether the action was successful or not

columns.name: ObjectId
columns.description: The name of the object that was modified by the activity

columns.name: UserId
columns.description: The UPN of the user who performed the action

columns.name: ClientIP
columns.description: The IP address of the device that was used when the activity was logged

columns.name: Scope
columns.description: Was this event created by a hosted Microsoft 365 service or an on-premises server

columns.name: CopilotEventData
columns.description: Copilot-specific event data including AI system plugins, accessed resources, messages, and model transparency details

columns.name: AgentID
columns.description: Unique identifier of the Agent

columns.name: AgentName
columns.description: The name of the Agent

columns.name: AgentType
columns.description: The type of the Agent - Microsoft (1P), External (3P), Shared by users (Shared), Built by your org (External)

columns.name: UserAssignments
columns.description: Collection of GUID of Users and Groups associated with the agent activity

columns.name: ForAllUsers
columns.description: Whether the activity associated with the agent was captured for the entire organization or not

columns.name: Property
columns.description: The tenant wide setting name that was updated by the admin

columns.name: RemovedIdentities
columns.description: Collection of GUID of Users or Groups that were removed

columns.name: AddedIdentities
columns.description: Collection of GUID of Users or Groups that were added

columns.name: NewValue
columns.description: The new value of the setting

columns.name: OldValue
columns.description: The old value or the new setting

columns.name: Entity
columns.description: ProjectEntity the audit was for

columns.name: Action
columns.description: ProjectAction that was taken

columns.name: OnBehalfOfResId
columns.description: The resource Id the action was taken on behalf of

columns.name: CaseId
columns.description: The identity (GUID) of the eDiscovery case

columns.name: CaseName
columns.description: The name of the eDiscovery case

columns.name: Object1Id
columns.description: The ID of the object (for example, a search, hold, or review set) that was created, accessed, or changed

columns.name: Object1Name
columns.description: The name of the object (for example, a search, hold or review set) that was created, accessed, or changed

columns.name: Object1Type
columns.description: The type of the eDiscovery object that the user created, deleted, or modified

columns.name: Object2Id
columns.description: The ID of the object (for example, a search, hold, or review set) that was created, accessed, or changed

columns.name: Object2Name
columns.description: The name of the object (for example, a search, hold or review set) that was created, accessed, or changed

columns.name: Object2Type
columns.description: The type of the eDiscovery object that the user created, deleted, or modified

columns.name: StartTime
columns.description: The date and time in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) when the eDiscovery activity was started

columns.name: EndTime
columns.description: The date and time in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) when the eDiscovery activity was ended

columns.name: UserCancelled
columns.description: Whether the specific activity was cancelled by the user

columns.name: ItemIds
columns.description: Item IDs associated with the activity

columns.name: ItemNames
columns.description: Item names associated with the activity

columns.name: DataSources
columns.description: A list of source IDs, source names and locations associated to the activity

columns.name: QueryId
columns.description: The ID of the query associated with the activity

columns.name: QueryText
columns.description: The query text associated with the activity, such as a search statistic process or add to review process

columns.name: QueryFiles
columns.description: Input file names used to generate the query. This is specific to the search by file gesture

columns.name: Settings
columns.description: Settings applied to the eDiscovery activity

columns.name: ExtendedProperties
columns.description: Additional properties related to the eDiscovery activity

columns.name: ExportName
columns.description: Name of the eDiscovery export

columns.name: JobId
columns.description: The GUID of the eDiscovery process

columns.name: RecordNumber
columns.description: Used when an audit record is divided into multiple parts due to size. It indicates the sequence of each part within the total splits

columns.name: ClientRequestId
columns.description: A GUID that can be used to correlate this cmdlet with portal operations. This information is used only by Microsoft support

columns.name: CmdletVersion
columns.description: The build version of the cmdlet when it was run

columns.name: EffectiveOrganization
columns.description: The GUID for the organization impacted by the cmdlet. (Deprecated: This parameter will eventually stop appearing.)

columns.name: UserServicePlan
columns.description: The service plan assigned to the user who ran the cmdlet

columns.name: ClientApplication
columns.description: If the cmdlet was run by an application, as opposed to remote PowerShell, this field contains the application name

columns.name: Parameters
columns.description: The name and value for parameters that were used with the cmdlet that do not include personal data

columns.name: NonPiiParameters
columns.description: The name and value for parameters that were used with the cmdlet that include personal data. (Deprecated: This field will eventually stop appearing and its content will be merged with the Parameters field.)

columns.name: AlertId
columns.description: The Guid of the alert

columns.name: AlertType
columns.description: Type of the alert. Alert types include: System, Custom

columns.name: Name
columns.description: Name of the alert

columns.name: PolicyId
columns.description: The Guid of the policy that triggered the alert

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Status of the alert. Statuses include: Active, Investigating, Resolved, Dismissed

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: Severity of the alert. Severity levels include: Low, Medium, High

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Category of the alert. Categories include: AccessGovernance, DataGovernance, DataLossPrevention, InsiderRiskManagement, MailFlow, ThreatManagement, Other

columns.name: Source
columns.description: Source of the alert. Sources include: Office 365 Security & Compliance, Cloud App Security

columns.name: Comments
columns.description: Comments left by the users who have viewed the alert. By default, it's 'New alert'

columns.name: Data
columns.description: The detailed data blob of the alert or alert entity

columns.name: AlertEntityId
columns.description: The identifier for the alert entity. This parameter is only applicable to AlertEntityGenerated events

columns.name: EntityType
columns.description: Type of the alert or alert entity. Entity types include: User, Recipients, Sender, MalwareFamily

columns.name: ActorUserId
columns.description: Email of user that performed the operation

columns.name: ActorYammerUserId
columns.description: ID of user that performed the operation

columns.name: DataExportType
columns.description: Returns 'data' if data export includes messages, notes, files, topics, users and groups; returns 'user' if data export includes users only

columns.name: FileId
columns.description: ID of the file in the operation

columns.name: FileName
columns.description: Name of the file in the operation. Appears blank if not relevant to the operation

columns.name: GroupName
columns.description: Name of the group in the operation. Appears blank if not relevant to the operation

columns.name: IsSoftDelete
columns.description: Returns 'true' if the network's data retention policy is set to Soft Delete; returns 'false' if the network's data retention policy is set to Hard Delete

columns.name: MeetingId
columns.description: The ID of the event/teams meeting in the operation

columns.name: MessageId
columns.description: ID of the message in the operation

columns.name: ModifiedProperties
columns.description: Includes the name of the property that was modified, the new value of the modified object and the previous value of the modified object

columns.name: YammerNetworkId
columns.description: Network ID of the user that performed the operation

columns.name: TargetObjectId
columns.description: Entra Id of the target user in the operation

columns.name: TargetUserId
columns.description: Email of target user in the operation. Appears blank if not relevant to the operation

columns.name: TargetYammerUserId
columns.description: ID of target user in the operation

columns.name: ThreadId
columns.description: ID of the Message thread in the operation

columns.name: VersionId
columns.description: Version ID of the file in the operation

columns.name: AttachmentData
columns.description: Data about attachments in the email message that triggered the event

columns.name: DetectionType
columns.description: The type of detection. For example: Inline, Detected at delivery, Delayed: Detected after delivery, ZAP: Removed after delivery by zero-hour auto purge (ZAP). Events with ZAP detection type are typically preceded by a message with a Delayed detection type

columns.name: DetectionMethod
columns.description: The detection method or technology used by Defender for Office 365

columns.name: InternetMessageId
columns.description: The Internet Message Id

columns.name: NetworkMessageId
columns.description: The Exchange Online Network Message Id

columns.name: P1Sender
columns.description: The MAIL FROM address used in SMTP transmission of the email message between email servers (also known as the 5321.MailFrom address, P1 sender, or envelope sender)

columns.name: P2Sender
columns.description: The From address (also known as the 5322.From address or P2 sender) that's shown in email clients

columns.name: Policy
columns.description: The type of threat policy (for example, Anti-spam or Anti-phish) and related action type (for example, High Confidence Spam, Spam, or Phish) relevant to the email message

columns.name: Recipients
columns.description: An array of recipients of the email message

columns.name: SenderIp
columns.description: The IPv4 or IPv6 address that submitted the message

columns.name: Subject
columns.description: The subject line of the message

columns.name: Verdict
columns.description: The message verdict

columns.name: MessageTime
columns.description: Date and time in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) the email message was received or sent

columns.name: EventDeepLink
columns.description: Deep-link to the email event in Threat Explorer or Real-time detections

columns.name: DeliveryAction
columns.description: The original delivery action on the email message

columns.name: OriginalDeliverylocation
columns.description: The original delivery location of the email message

columns.name: LatestDeliverylocation
columns.description: The latest delivery location of the email message at the time of the event

columns.name: Directionality
columns.description: Identifies whether an email message was inbound, outbound, or an intra-org message

columns.name: ThreatsAndDetectionTech
columns.description: The threats and the corresponding detection technologies. This field exposes all the threats on an email message, including the latest addition on spam verdict

columns.name: AdditionalActionsAndResults
columns.description: The additional actions that were taken on the email, such as ZAP or Manual Remediation. Also includes the corresponding results

columns.name: Connectors
columns.description: The names and GUIDs of the connectors associated with the email

columns.name: AuthDetails
columns.description: The authentication checks that are done for the email. Also includes the values for SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and CompAuth

columns.name: SystemOverrides
columns.description: Overrides that are applicable to the email. These can be system or user overrides

columns.name: PhishConfidenceLevel
columns.description: Indicates the confidence level associated with Phish verdict. It can be Normal or High

columns.name: BatchID
columns.description: A unique identifier for a group of records that are processed together

columns.name: CampaignID
columns.description: A unique identifier for the attack simulation training campaign

columns.name: UserDisplayName
columns.description: The display name of the user involved in the attack simulation training campaign

columns.name: AttackTechnique
columns.description: The attack technique used in the simulation

columns.name: CampaignType
columns.description: The type of attack simulation campaign. The different types are simulation campaign, training campaign, simulation automation

columns.name: TimeData
columns.description: Campaign launch time

columns.name: EndTimeData
columns.description: Campaign end time

columns.name: AttackSimEvent
columns.description: Type of event (user activity or message delivery state)

columns.name: AttackSimAdminEvent
columns.description: The type of attack sim admin event

columns.name: CourseID
columns.description: A unique identifier for a training course

columns.name: UserTrainingEvent
columns.description: The type of user training event

columns.name: AdminSubmissionRegistered
columns.description: Admin submission is registered and is pending for processing

columns.name: AdminSubmissionDeliveryCheck
columns.description: Admin submission system checked the email's policy

columns.name: AdminSubmissionSubmitting
columns.description: Admin submission system is submitting the email

columns.name: AdminSubmissionSubmitted
columns.description: Admin submission system submitted the email

columns.name: AdminSubmissionTriage
columns.description: Admin submission triaged by grader

columns.name: AdminSubmissionTimeout
columns.description: Admin submission timed out with no result

columns.name: UserSubmission
columns.description: Submission was first reported by an end user

columns.name: UserSubmissionTriage
columns.description: User submission is triaged by grader

columns.name: CustomSubmission
columns.description: Message reported by a user was sent to the organization's custom mailbox as set in the user reported messages settings

columns.name: AttackSimUserSubmission
columns.description: The user-reported message was actually a phish simulation training message

columns.name: AdminSubmissionTablAllow
columns.description: An allow entry in the Tenant Allow/Block List was created at time of submission to immediately take action on similar messages while it is being rescanned

columns.name: SubmissionNotification
columns.description: Admin feedback is sent to end user

columns.name: InvestigationId
columns.description: Investigation ID/GUID

columns.name: InvestigationName
columns.description: Name of the investigation

columns.name: InvestigationType
columns.description: Type of the investigation. Can take one of the following values: User-Reported Messages, Zapped Malware, Zapped Phish, Url Verdict Change

columns.name: LastUpdateTimeUtc
columns.description: UTC time of the last update for an investigation

columns.name: StartTimeUtc
columns.description: Start time for an investigation

columns.name: DeeplinkURL
columns.description: Deep link URL to an investigation in the Microsoft Defender portal

columns.name: Actions
columns.description: Collection of actions recommended by an investigation

columns.name: ActionType
columns.description: The type of the action, such as email remediation

columns.name: ActionStatus
columns.description: Values include: Pending, Running, Waiting on resource, Completed, Failed

columns.name: ApprovedBy
columns.description: Null if auto approved; otherwise, the username/id

columns.name: TimestampUtc
columns.description: The timestamp of the action status change

columns.name: ActionId
columns.description: Unique identifier for action

columns.name: RelatedAlertIds
columns.description: Alerts related to an investigation

columns.name: EndTimeUtc
columns.description: Action final status update timestamp

columns.name: ResourceIdentifiers
columns.description: Consists of the Azure Active Directory tenant ID

columns.name: Entities
columns.description: List of one or more affected entities by action

columns.name: Audit
columns.description: System information related to the hygiene event

columns.name: Event
columns.description: The type of hygiene event. The values for this parameter are Listed or Delisted

columns.name: EventId
columns.description: The ID of the hygiene event type

columns.name: EventValue
columns.description: The user who was impacted

columns.name: Reason
columns.description: Details about the hygiene event

columns.name: AppName
columns.description: The name of the app where the event occurred

columns.name: DashboardName
columns.description: The name of the dashboard where the event occurred

columns.name: DataClassification
columns.description: The data classification, if any, for the dashboard where the event occurred

columns.name: DatasetName
columns.description: The name of the dataset where the event occurred

columns.name: MembershipInformation
columns.description: Membership information about the group

columns.name: OrgAppPermission
columns.description: Permissions list for an organizational app (entire organization, specific users, or specific groups)

columns.name: ReportName
columns.description: The name of the report where the event occurred

columns.name: SharingInformation
columns.description: Information about the person to whom a sharing invitation is sent

columns.name: SwitchState
columns.description: Information about the state of various tenant level switches

columns.name: WorkSpaceName
columns.description: The name of the workspace where the event occurred

columns.name: WpaUserRole
columns.description: The Viva Insights role of the user who performed the action

columns.name: OperationDetails
columns.description: A list of extended properties for the setting that was changed. Each property has a Name and Value

columns.name: RequestType
columns.description: The type of quarantine request performed by a user

columns.name: RequestSource
columns.description: Quarantine request come from the Microsoft Defender portal, a cmdlet, or a URLlink

columns.name: ReleaseTo
columns.description: The recipient of the email message

columns.name: FormsUserTypes
columns.description: The role of the user who performed the action. The values for this parameter are Admin, Owner, Responder, or Coauthor

columns.name: SourceApp
columns.description: Indicates if the action is from Forms website or from another App

columns.name: FormName
columns.description: The name of the current form

columns.name: FormId
columns.description: The Id of the target form

columns.name: FormTypes
columns.description: Indicates whether this is a Form, Quiz, or Survey

columns.name: ActivityParameters
columns.description: JSON string containing activity parameters. For more information, see Microsoft Forms activities

columns.name: Sender
columns.description: The email address in the From field of the email message

columns.name: Receivers
columns.description: All email addresses in the To, CC, and Bcc fields of the email message

columns.name: ItemName
columns.description: The string in the Subject field of the email message

columns.name: LabelId
columns.description: The GUID of the sensitivity label applied to the email message

columns.name: LabelName
columns.description: The name of the sensitivity label applied to the email message

columns.name: LabelAction
columns.description: The actions specified by the sensitivity label that were applied to the email message before the message entered the mail transport pipeline

columns.name: LabelAppliedDateTime
columns.description: The date the sensitivity label was applied to the email message

columns.name: ApplicationMode
columns.description: Specifies how the sensitivity label was applied to the email message. The Privileged value indicates the label was manually applied by a user. The Standard value indicates the label was auto-applied by a client-side or service-side labeling process

columns.name: Recipient
columns.description: Recipient email address

columns.name: AuthenticationMethod
columns.description: Authentication method when accessing the message, i.e. OTP, Yahoo, Gmail, Microsoft

columns.name: AuthenticationStatus
columns.description: 0: Success, 1: Failure

columns.name: OperationStatus
columns.description: 0: Success, 1: Failure

columns.name: AttachmentName
columns.description: Name of the attachment

columns.name: OperationProperties
columns.description: Extra properties, i.e. number of OTP passcode sent, email subject, etc.

columns.name: TaskId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the periodic data connector instance. Data connectors import data in periodic intervals

columns.name: ItemId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the item (for example, an email message) being imported

columns.name: ItemSize
columns.description: The size of the item being imported

columns.name: SourceUserId
columns.description: The unique identifier of the user from the third-party data source. For example, for a Slack data connector, this property specifies the user Id in Slack workspace

columns.name: FailureType
columns.description: Indicates the type of data import failure. For example, the value incorrectusermapping indicates the item wasn't imported because no user mapping between the third-party data source and Microsoft 365 could be found

columns.name: ResultMessage
columns.description: Indicates the type of failure, such as Duplicate message

columns.name: IsRetry
columns.description: Indicates whether the data connector retried to import the item

columns.name: Attachments
columns.description: A list of attachments received from the third-party data source

columns.name: DataStoreType
columns.description: Indicates which data store the data was downloaded from. Refer DataStoreType for all possible values

columns.name: UserAction
columns.description: Indicates what action user had performed on the data store. Refer DataLakeUserAction for all possible values

columns.name: ExportTriggeredAt
columns.description: Indicates when the data export was triggered

columns.name: NameOfDownloadedZipFile
columns.description: The name of the compressed file the admin had downloaded from the Data Lake

columns.name: Invitation
columns.description: Details of the invite sent to the recipient of the Data Share

columns.name: ClientUUID
columns.description: The Client UUID of the Viva Glint instance

columns.name: ImportType
columns.description: The data import source type

columns.name: DataDSRControl
columns.description: This in-platform control determines whether survey data is deleted when a user is removed from the Viva Glint platform

columns.name: DiscardEmployeeIds
columns.description: This in-platform control specifies whether the Employee IDs of previously deleted employees are disregarded or retained in the Viva Glint platform

columns.name: ExtendedCompletionDate
columns.description: The new date to which a closed survey cycle has been extended

columns.name: FeedBackComponentName
columns.description: The name of a specific component within the 360 feedback program

columns.name: Detail
columns.description: A description of the event or the activity that occurred in Viva Goals

columns.name: Username
columns.description: The name of the user who triggered the event

columns.name: UserRole
columns.description: The role of the user who triggered this event in Viva Goals. This value mentions if the user is an organization admin or an owner

columns.name: OrganizationName
columns.description: The name of the organization in Viva Goals where the event was triggered

columns.name: OrganizationOwner
columns.description: The owner of the organization in Viva Goals where the event occurred

columns.name: OrganizationAdmins
columns.description: The admin(s) of the organization in Viva Goals where the event occurred. There can be one or more admins in the organization

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: The user agent (browser details) of the user who triggered the event. UserAgent might not be present in case of a system generated event

columns.name: ModifiedFields
columns.description: A list of attributes that were modified along with its new and old values output as a JSON

columns.name: ItemDetails
columns.description: Additional properties about the object that was modified

columns.name: EditMethodology
columns.description: How the policy was created / edited

columns.name: CountOfArtifactsBeingAdded
columns.description: Number of artifacts being added

columns.name: CountOfArtifactsBeingRemoved
columns.description: Number of artifacts being removed

columns.name: ServiceType
columns.description: Whether it is a SharePoint, Exchange, or OneDriveForBusiness policy

columns.name: CreationMethodology
columns.description: How the Restore Task was created / edited

columns.name: RestoreTime
columns.description: Time which the item is being restored to

columns.name: RestoreLocationType
columns.description: Location type that the item is being restored to

columns.name: RestoreLocation
columns.description: Location that the item is being restored to

columns.name: BackupItemID
columns.description: ID of the Backup Item being restored

columns.name: ProtectionUnitID
columns.description: Protection Unit ID of the item being restored

columns.name: SuccessStatus
columns.description: Whether the restore operation was successful

columns.name: BackupItemType
columns.description: Whether the Backup Item is a Site / Account / Mailbox

columns.name: URLPath
columns.description: The URL that was browsed

columns.name: DomainURL
columns.description: The domain URL that was browsed

columns.name: ScenarioType
columns.description: The name of automation

columns.name: PromptText
columns.description: The Copilot prompt that's run by the LLM

columns.name: AutomationId
columns.description: A unique identifier that correlates all activities related to each run of the Copilot prompt

columns.name: TriggerMode
columns.description: The schedule for when the Copilot prompt runs, shown in cron format

columns.name: PlaceType
columns.description: The type for the place item that is created/updated/deleted by the request. For example, 'Building', 'Room', 'Desk', and so on

columns.name: EventTime
columns.description: Timestamp when the Spark Notebook execution was submitted/started

columns.name: Compute
columns.description: Compute selected

columns.name: DatabaseName
columns.description: The workspace the command ran on

columns.name: TableName
columns.description: Name of the table

columns.name: SessionDurationInSecs
columns.description: Total duration of the session

columns.name: SessionStartTime
columns.description: Start time for the session

columns.name: SessionEndTime
columns.description: End time for the session

columns.name: KernalId
columns.description: Jupyter KernelId, uniquely identifies the Notebook session

columns.name: SessionId
columns.description: Correlation ID for the various code blocks executed with a Spark session

columns.name: Interface
columns.description: Interface from where the notebook was run

columns.name: JobType
columns.description: Type of Job like Notebook, KQL

columns.name: JobName
columns.description: Name of the job

columns.name: Schedule
columns.description: Schedule details, if configured for the job

columns.name: RunID
columns.description: ID of a specific job run instance

columns.name: JobRunStatus
columns.description: Status of the job execution

columns.name: Logs
columns.description: Logs from the notebook run

columns.name: JobExecutionDurationInSecs
columns.description: Time taken for job execution

columns.name: JobTotalDurationInSecs
columns.description: Total Duration of the job operation including session

columns.name: JobStartTime
columns.description: Start time of the job

columns.name: JobEndTime
columns.description: End time of the job

columns.name: DatabasesRead
columns.description: The list of workspaces read by the job

columns.name: DatabasesWrite
columns.description: The list of workspaces written to by the job

columns.name: TablesRead
columns.description: The list of tables read by the job

columns.name: TablesWrite
columns.description: The list of tables written to by the job

columns.name: Query
columns.description: KQL query or notebook executed in the job

columns.name: ResultTableCount
columns.description: Output Table Count

columns.name: QueryResponse
columns.description: Response from executing the KQL query

columns.name: TotalRows
columns.description: Total Rows returned from query execution. List of values if multiple queries executed at once

columns.name: ComponentFault
columns.description: The entity that caused the query to fail. For example, if the query result is too large, the ComponentFault is 'Client'. If an internal error occurred, the ComponentFault is 'Server'

columns.name: FailureReason
columns.description: If KQL query failed, the reason for failure

columns.name: ExecutionDuration
columns.description: Time taken for query execution, in milliseconds

columns.name: TotalCPU
columns.description: Total CPU Duration of the run

columns.name: MemoryPeak
columns.description: Memory Peak of the KQL query execution

columns.name: BillingAzureSubscriptionId
columns.description: Azure subscription chosen for Microsoft Sentinel data lake billing

columns.name: BillingAzureResourceGroupName
columns.description: Azure resource group chosen for Microsoft Sentinel data lake billing

columns.name: ProvisioningStatus
columns.description: Status of provisioning the lake

columns.name: DataOnboardingAtSetup
columns.description: Data sets ingested during Microsoft Sentinel data lake onboarding

columns.name: Tables
columns.description: List of table names ingested during Microsoft Sentinel data lake onboarding

columns.name: SubscriptionsEnabled
columns.description: List of subscriptions enabled for ARG ingestion

columns.name: DataOnboardingStatus
columns.description: Status of operation

columns.name: EventOccurenceTime
columns.description: Timestamp of the operation

columns.name: ToolID
columns.description: Identifier of the AI Tool

columns.name: ToolName
columns.description: Name of the AI Tool

columns.name: InputParameters
columns.description: Parameters given to the tool

columns.name: APIsCalled
columns.description: APIs called by the AI Tool

columns.name: DataScanned
columns.description: Total GBs Scanned

columns.name: TotalCpuHours
columns.description: Total CPU Duration of the run

columns.name: TotalSCUHours
columns.description: Total SCUs used in the run

columns.name: GraphName
columns.description: Name of the graph instance

columns.name: OperationInput
columns.description: Parameters of graph action

columns.name: GraphQueryStats
columns.description: Collection of response metadata

columns.name: GraphQueryStatus
columns.description: Response of the query or action on graph

columns.name: DataCenterSecurityEventType
columns.description: The type of cmdlet event in lock box

columns.name: ElevationTime
columns.description: The start time of the elevation

columns.name: ElevationApprover
columns.description: The name of a Microsoft manager

columns.name: ElevationApprovedTime
columns.description: The timestamp for when the elevation was approved

columns.name: ElevationRequestId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the elevation request

columns.name: ElevationRole
columns.description: The role the elevation was requested for

columns.name: ElevationDuration
columns.description: The duration for which the elevation was active

columns.name: GenericInfo
columns.description: Used for comments and other generic information

columns.name: EventName
columns.description: A description of the event or the activity that occurred in Viva Pulse

columns.name: PulseId
columns.description: Id of the pulse survey

columns.name: EventDetails
columns.description: Additional properties about the event

columns.name: SharePointMetaData
columns.description: Describes metadata about the document in SharePoint or OneDrive that contained the sensitive information

columns.name: ExchangeMetaData
columns.description: Describes metadata about the email message that contained the sensitive information

columns.name: EndpointMetaData
columns.description: Describes metadata about the document in endpoint that contained the sensitive information

columns.name: ExceptionInfo
columns.description: Identifies reasons why a policy no longer applies and/or any information about false positive and/or override noted by the end user

columns.name: PolicyDetails
columns.description: Information about 1 or more policies that triggered the DLP event

columns.name: SensitiveInfoDetectionIsIncluded
columns.description: Indicates whether the event contains the value of the sensitive data type and surrounding context from the source content

columns.name: EvaluationSource
columns.description: DLP evaluation source (e.g., DlpPolicyEventBasedAssistantOneDriveForBusiness)

columns.name: IncidentId
columns.description: DLP incident identifier (GUID)
